title: /kernel:run
summary_text: Consistency check result for `/kernel:run` bootstrap receipt `kernel-20260327T1254Z`: 1. PASS: Receipt exists and is bound to run id `kernel-20260327T1254Z` at `/Users/masayuki/.config/kernel/bootstrap-receipts/kernel-20260327T1254Z.json`. 2. PASS: `lane_count: 6` and `manifest_lane_count: 6` are internally aligned; `mode: normal` is encoded consistently. 3. PASS: Required provider roles are present in receipt fields (`has_codex: true`, `has_glm: true`, `specialist_count: 1`). 4. PARTIAL: `providers` contains duplicate `codex` entries (`codex` repeated 4×), which is internally valid JSON-wise but can only represent collapsed lane roles if that was intentional; it does not itself prove 6 materially distinct lane manifests. 5. PASS/CAUTION: `active_models` includes 3 entries and count matches (`active_model_count: 3`), but manifest-specific fields (`[lane name]/[role]/[focus]/[agent]/[subagent]`) are not stored in this receipt file. Bottom line: receipt metadata is internally self-consistent; claim coverage for a truthful 6-lane manifest still depends on externally maintained live lane evidence, not just this file.
